Function.Name: ReadSystemStatus
Function.Desc: Command to read status information from DLP Controller. If status interrupt is enabled (configurable via default UI tool in DLP Composer), reading back this command will acknowledge/deactivate the interrupt pin until the next change in status.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: ReadEepromDataPresent
Function.Desc: Reports which of the calibration data blocks are present in EEPROM. Use this command before sending EEPROM Invalidate command (0x0A).

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: WriteGeneralDelayCommand
Function.Desc: On receipt of this command controller wait for specified period before executing the next command. This command to be used in Auto Initialization batchfile configuration. Use this command to insert delay between execution of two commands.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: DelayInMilliseconds
Param.Desc: Delay In Milliseconds

Function.Name: WriteEepromInvalidate
Function.Desc: Invalidates the user settings portion of EEPROM data or calibration portion of EEPROM data or both as per input arguments and restarts the system. If none of the settings or calibration data is selected, then the command does nothing. Note: Chose valid flags as returned in Get EEPROM Data Present command.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: WriteMemoryArray
Function.Desc: Writes a stream of words into the RAM memory (DRAM or IRAM) starting from the address specified. Performs no checks whether the specified memory address given is valid.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: ReadMemoryArray
Function.Desc: Reads a stream of words from memory starting from the address specified. Performs no checks whether the specified memory address given is valid.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Data
Param.Desc: Data

Param.Name: StartAddress
Param.Desc: Start Address from which data is to be read

Param.Name: AddressIncrement
Param.Desc: Address increment steps. 0 - No increment

Param.Name: AccessWidth
Param.Desc: Read access width

Param.Name: NumberOfWords
Param.Desc: Number of words to be read

Param.Name: NumberOfBytesPerWord
Param.Desc: The number of bytes per word. The range is 1 to 4.

Function.Name: WriteDisplay
Function.Desc: Displays the specified source. Note: If Display External projection mode is selected and if there is no source present it will show Splash or Solid Field depending on the default settings in the system.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Source
Param.Desc: Source

Function.Name: ReadDisplay
Function.Desc: Returns the source which is currently being displayed.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Source
Param.Desc: The source currently being displayed.

Function.Name: ReadBootHoldReason
Function.Desc: Returns the code that specifies the reason for being in bootloader mode.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ReasonCode
Param.Desc: Reason code 0x00 BOOT_HOLD jumper in HOLD position 0x01 Switched to programming mode initated by main app 0x02 Reading flash info failed 0x03 Flash layout mismatch 0x04 Can't initialize ARM peripherals 0x05 Can't allocate memory pool 0x06 Failure in initialization task 0x07 Controller is invalid to run the application 0x08 Error in USB initialization 0x09 Error in i2c initialization 0x0A Error getting app configuration 0x0B App configuration layout mismatch

Function.Name: WriteEnableLowLatencyMode
Function.Desc: Enables or disables the Low latency mode of operation in which processing delay (from the input source to the frame sent to DMD) by the Controller is limited to a maximum of one and a half frame delays.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: 1 = Low latency mode is enabled 0 = Low latency mode is disabled

Function.Name: ReadEnableLowLatencyMode
Function.Desc: Returns whether low latency mode is enabled or not.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: 0 = Low latency mode is disabled; 1 = Low latency mode is enabled.

Function.Name: WriteSystemLook
Function.Desc: This command sets the current system look. System looks shall be designed and configured via DLP Composer tool. System look determines the current group of sequences and color points to be loaded. This command also initiates the source definition change that corresponds to new look index.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: System
Param.Desc: Look Index

Function.Name: ReadSystemLook
Function.Desc: This command gets the current system look.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: System
Param.Desc: Look Index

Function.Name: WriteTpgPredefinedPattern
Function.Desc: This command will set one of the pre-defined test patterns stored in flash (configured using DLP Composer tool). The function selects a pattern to load from flash into the test pattern generator hardware. The information retrieved from the flash includes pattern definition, color definition, and the resolution. The pre-defined patterns are included in the flash configuration data. Set Display command must be called to switch the display mode from other modes to TPG prior to or after this command .

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: PatternNumber
Param.Desc: Predefined test pattern number to be displayed

Function.Name: ReadTpgPredefinedPattern
Function.Desc: Returns the current selection for pre-defined test patterns.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: PatternNumber
Param.Desc: Predefined test pattern number selected

Function.Name: WriteTpgBorder
Function.Desc: Draws a border around the test pattern of given width and color. Note: To be used only when the Display is set as Test Pattern.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Width
Param.Desc: Width of the Border. The range is 0 to 20.

Param.Name: BorderColorRed
Param.Desc: Border Color Red Value. The range is 0 to 1023.

Param.Name: BorderColorGreen
Param.Desc: Border Color Green Value. The range is 0 to 1023.

Param.Name: BorderColorBlue
Param.Desc: Border Color Blue Value. The range is 0 to 1023.

Function.Name: ReadTpgBorder
Function.Desc: Returns Width in number of pixels and Color of Border for a test Pattern.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Width
Param.Desc: Width of the Border. The range is 0 to 20.

Param.Name: BorderColorRed
Param.Desc: Border Color Red Value. The range is 0 to 1023.

Param.Name: BorderColorGreen
Param.Desc: Border Color Green Value. The range is 0 to 1023.

Param.Name: BorderColorBlue
Param.Desc: Border Color Blue Value. The range is 0 to 1023.

Function.Name: WriteTpgResolution
Function.Desc: Sets horizontal and vertical resolution in number of pixels for current test pattern.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: HorizontalResolution
Param.Desc: Horizontal resolution of test pattern. The range is 640 to 4096.

Param.Name: VerticalResolution
Param.Desc: Vertical resolution of test pattern. The range is 480 to 2400.

Function.Name: ReadTpgResolution
Function.Desc: Returns horizontal and vertical resolution in number of pixels for current test pattern.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: HorizontalResolution
Param.Desc: Horizontal resolution of test pattern. The range is 640 to 4096.

Param.Name: VerticalResolution
Param.Desc: Vertical resolution of test pattern. The range is 480 to 2400.

Function.Name: WriteTpgFrameRate
Function.Desc: Sets frame rate in Hz for current test pattern.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: FrameRate
Param.Desc: Frame rate of test pattern(Hz). The range is 30 to 240.

Function.Name: ReadTpgFrameRate
Function.Desc: Returns frame rate in Hz for current test pattern.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: FrameRate
Param.Desc: Frame rate of test pattern. The range is 30 to 240.

Function.Name: WriteSfgColor
Function.Desc: Configures the solid color to be displayed when display is set to solid field generator (SFG). This command only sets the SFG color and does NOT display it. In order to display the SFG, Display needs to be set with SFG as source(Use Set Display command for this.)

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Red
Param.Desc: Red color level.The range is 0 to 1023.

Param.Name: Green
Param.Desc: Green color level.The range is 0 to 1023.

Param.Name: Blue
Param.Desc: Blue color level.The range is 0 to 1023.

Function.Name: ReadSfgColor
Function.Desc: Returns the solid color which is programmed to be displayed when display is set to SFG.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Red
Param.Desc: Red color level.The range is 0 to 1023.

Param.Name: Green
Param.Desc: Green color level.The range is 0 to 1023.

Param.Name: Blue
Param.Desc: Blue color level.The range is 0 to 1023.

Function.Name: ReadSfgResolution
Function.Desc: Gets the resolution of the displayed SFG image.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: HorizontalResolution
Param.Desc: Horizontal resolution of SFG(Pixels). The range is 0 to 4096.

Param.Name: VerticalResolution
Param.Desc: Vertical resolution of SFG(Lines). The range is 0 to 2160.

Function.Name: WriteCurtainColor
Function.Desc: Command to set the color to be used in curtain mode. Use Set Display command to switch to curtain mode.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Color
Param.Desc: The background color to be set as curtain.

Function.Name: ReadCurtainColor
Function.Desc: Command that returns the color used in curtain mode.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Color
Param.Desc: The background color set as curtain. This returns the background colorof the display even if the curtain is not enabled.

Function.Name: WriteSplashLoadImage
Function.Desc: Sets the index of the splash image to be loaded and displayed. If already in Splash mode the requested splash image is displayed.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Index
Param.Desc: The 0-based index of Splash Image (0xff for captured splash).The range is 0 to 255.

Function.Name: ReadSplashLoadImage
Function.Desc: Gets the index of the splash image to be loaded and displayed.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Index
Param.Desc: The 0-based index of Splash Image (0xff for captured splash).The range is 0 to 255.

Function.Name: WriteEnableImageFlip
Function.Desc: Flips the data output to the display vertically or horizontally. This feature is provided to support use cases like ceiling mount, rear projection etc.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: VertFlip
Param.Desc: 0 = Vertical Flip of the image is disabled; 1 = Vertical Flip of the image is enabled.

Param.Name: HorzFlip
Param.Desc: 0 = Horizontal Flip of the image is disabled; 1 = Horizontal Flip of the image is enabled.

Function.Name: ReadEnableImageFlip
Function.Desc: Returns whether image flipping is enabled.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: VertFlip
Param.Desc: 0 = Vertical Flip of the image is disabled; 1 = Vertical Flip of the image is enabled.

Param.Name: HorzFlip
Param.Desc: 0 = Horizontal Flip of the image is disabled; 1 = Horizontal Flip of the image is enabled.

Function.Name: WriteEnableFreeze
Function.Desc: It enables or disables display freeze which freezes the current frame being displayed on the screen. Caution: Set Curtain or any operation that requires curtain will override Freeze and frozen image on the wall will be lost. The following operations require curtain (and will override Freeze): Source Type Switch (Standard - XPR - 3D) Source Type Switch (interlaced - non-interlaced) Switch to Splash Display Splash Capture Low Latency Mode Switch Source Relocking Switch to Stand-By/Low-Power mode

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Freeze
Param.Desc: 0 = Display freeze is disabled; 1 = Display freeze is enabled.

Function.Name: ReadEnableFreeze
Function.Desc: Returns whether the current display is frozen.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Freeze
Param.Desc: 1 = Display freeze is enabled 0 = Display freeze is disabled

Function.Name: WriteKeystoneAngles
Function.Desc: Configures the Keystone correction when the pitch, yaw, roll, throw ratio and vertical offset of corrected image are known. Keystone correction is used to remove the distortion caused when the projector is not orthogonal to the projection surface (screen). Keystone feature will be automatically enabled when this command is executed. Note: The actual range of these parameters depends on the light engine (projection optics); the range of Pitch, Yaw and Roll is derived from optical engine Vertical offset and Throw Ratio.(Maximum range: -40 to +40 degrees)

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Pitch
Param.Desc: Pitch angle in degrees. The range is -128 to 127.9960375.

Param.Name: Yaw
Param.Desc: Yaw angle in degrees Set to 0 for 1D correction. The range is -128 to 127.9960375.

Param.Name: Roll
Param.Desc: Roll angle in degrees Set to 0 for 1D/2D correction. The range is -128 to 127.9960375.

Function.Name: ReadKeystoneAngles
Function.Desc: Returns the keystone configuration parameters currently set.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Pitch
Param.Desc: Pitch. The range is -128 to 127.9960375.

Param.Name: Yaw
Param.Desc: Yaw. The range is -128 to 127.9960375.

Param.Name: Roll
Param.Desc: Roll. The range is -128 to 127.9960375.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ThrowRatio
Param.Desc: Throw Ratio. The range is 0.15 to 2.

Param.Name: VerticalOffset
Param.Desc: Vertical Offset. The range is -1.5 to 1.5.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ThrowRatio
Param.Desc: Throw Ratio. The range is 0.15 to 2.

Param.Name: VerticalOffset
Param.Desc: Vertical Offset. The range is -1.5 to 1.5.

Function.Name: ReadFlashInfo
Function.Desc: This command returns the flash device and manufacturer IDs. Only CFI compliant flash devices are supported. The system can have multiple flash devices. The command returns the info for the flash present at the given chip select. Note: Chip Select 0 Flash is required for system operation. Other Flash Chip Selects are technically optional but required for Splash Capture and Warp Operations.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ManfId
Param.Desc: Manufacturer ID

Param.Name: DevId
Param.Desc: Device ID

Param.Name: DevSize
Param.Desc: Device size in bytes

Param.Name: SectorInfo
Param.Desc: Sector Information

Param.Name: AvailableForProgramming
Param.Desc: 0 = Flash can be used for programming; 1 = Flash cannot be used for programming.

Param.Name: ChipSelect
Param.Desc: Chip Select of Flash Device

Function.Name: WriteEnableAnamorphicScaling
Function.Desc: Enables or disables the anamorphic scaling

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: 0 = Anamorphic Scaling is disabled; 1 = Anamorphic Scaling is enabled.

Function.Name: ReadEnableAnamorphicScaling
Function.Desc: Returns whether anamorphic scaling is enabled or not.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: 0 = Anamorphic Scaling is disabled; 1 = Anamorphic Scaling is enabled.

Function.Name: ReadProgrammableFlashSectorInformation
Function.Desc: This command returns the flash sector information read from CFI compliant flash devices. If the flash is non-CFI compliant, this command will fail. The sectors returned by this command are the only ones available for programming a flash image. The system is designed such that the flash image is in a contiguous memory space. If a system has multiple flash parts, then the software checks the size of the flash at ChipSelect 0. If this is equal to the maximum supported size (32MB), then a flash device at ChipSelect 1 (if present) will also be supported for flash programming. Similarly, if the size of flash devices at both ChipSelect 0 and 1 are 32MB, then a flash device at ChipSelect 2 (if present) will be supported for flash programming as well. The command appends the sector information for each part, which is supported for flash programming, and provides them as output.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: SectorInfo
Param.Desc: Sector Information

Function.Name: WriteDisplayImageSize
Function.Desc: Configures the cropping of input image and resizing of image that is displayed. Cropped area can be equal to or less than the input image size. The display area has has to be within DMD effective number of pixels and lines. Note: 1. Cropped Area and Display Area parameters are valid only when image size type is set to Manual. 2. For TPG, SFG and Splash, Cropped Area parameter is ignored. For those sources, cropped area is automatically set as explained below: a. For TPG, cropped area is set to TPG resolution. b. For Splash, cropped area is set to Splash image size. c. For SFG, cropped area is set to SFG resolution which is equal to source area of last stable external source or TPG.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: ReadDisplayImageSize
Function.Desc: Returns current image size, cropping and display settings.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: WriteUnlockFlashForUpdate
Function.Desc: This command unlocks the flash update operation (Download, Erase). By default the flash update operations are locked. This is to prevent accidental modification of flash contents. To unlock, the pre-defined key shall be send as the unlock code. Calling this command with any other parameter will lock the flash update commands.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Unlock
Param.Desc: Flash Update lock/unlock

Function.Name: ReadUnlockFlashForUpdate
Function.Desc: This command returns whether the flash is in unlocked state.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: IsUnlocked
Param.Desc: 0 = Locked 1 = Unlocked

Function.Name: WriteSourceConfiguration
Function.Desc: Configures the characteristics of the source on the Current active port. Note: 1. After sending CMD_SetSourceConfiguration command, CMD_SetDisplayImageSize command must be sent for the changes to take effect. 2. CSC will take effect only after sending the CMD_SetDisplayImageSize command. 3. CMD_SetSourceConfiguration command should not be used when the Display is set as TPG.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: ReadSourceConfiguration
Function.Desc: Retrieves the source characteristics for the current active port.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: WriteEraseSector
Function.Desc: This command erases the sector of the flash where the given address falls in. This command is a flash update command, and requires flash operations to be unlocked using Unlock Flash for Update command. The sector address shall be specified as an offset from flash start address. For example in a flash device where all sectors are 64KB of size, sector addresses shall be specified as follows: Sector 0 = 0 Sector 1 = 0x10000 Sector 2 = 0x20000 and so on...

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: SectorAddress
Param.Desc: Sector Address

Function.Name: WriteInitializeFlashReadWriteSettings
Function.Desc: This command initializes flash read/write operation. This command shall be called before Flash Write command is sent. Note: For Flash Write, the Address and download size set up shall both be even.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: StartAddress
Param.Desc: Start Address offset to program data to where Offset 0 refers to first byte in the flash, 1 refers to second byte and so on. This offset must be an even number.

Param.Name: NumBytes
Param.Desc: This specifies the number of bytes Flash Write command should expect or the number of bytes Flash Read command should return.This must be an even number.

Function.Name: WriteAutolockControl
Function.Desc: This command provides user control to relock to a source or to start/stop autolock algorithm.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: AutolockControl
Param.Desc: Autolock Control

Function.Name: WriteFlashWrite
Function.Desc: This command is used to program data to flash. This command shall be called only after setting the start address and size using the Initialize Flash Read/Write Settings command. This command is a flash update command, and requires flash operations to be unlocked using Unlock Flash for Update command. Flash write commands can be chained till the initialized number of bytes are programmed. The bootloader will auto-increment the address and size for each command. Only the initialized number of bytes will be programmed even if more data is provided. It is important to send only even number of bytes per flash write command to ensure all bytes are written. This is done so that all flash writes are optimized as per the multi-word write supported by the flash device. This command supports variable sized payload.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Data
Param.Desc: Data to write to flash memory

Function.Name: ReadFlashWrite
Function.Desc: This command is used to read data from flash. This command shall be called only after setting the start address and size using the Initialize Flash Read/Write Settings command. Flash read commands can be chained until the initialized number of bytes are returned. The bootloader will auto-increment the address and size for each command. Only the initialized number of bytes will be returned. Calling the function after returning requested data will return in command failure. This command supports variable sized response.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Data
Param.Desc: The bytes read from the flash

Param.Name: NumBytesToRead
Param.Desc: Num bytes to read in this command

Param.Name: SumofSumChecksum

Param.Name: StartAddress
Param.Desc: Start Address offset for checksum computation where Offset 0 refers to first byte in the flash, 1 refers to second byte and so on.

Param.Name: NumBytes
Param.Desc: Number of bytes to compute checksum

Function.Name: WriteBlendMapGainValues
Function.Desc: This command takes input from the user of Gain values of control points as part of the Blend Map

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: CompressionEnabled
Param.Desc: 1 = Compressed data is passed below for Gain values. 0 = Uncompressed data is passed below for Gain values.

Param.Name: ColorChannelSelect

Param.Name: TotalNumberOfCompressedValues
Param.Desc: Here the total number of compressed values needs to be passed only when CompressionEnable is Enabled otherwise pass 0 here

Param.Desc: Starting Index for filling the Gain values in array of Gain

Param.Name: GainValues
Param.Desc: Gain of control points .The format of input should be such that required gain which is a value between 0 to 1.99 be multiplied by 4096 before passing it in command.

Param.Name: Index

Param.Name: NoOfValues

Function.Name: WriteManualWarpTable
Function.Desc: This command writes to the warp map table that can be enabled using the Set Apply Manual Warping command. N warp map points (that does not exceed the command packet size) can be loaded at a time to anywhere within the table. Maximum number of points that can be set using this command is 62 in the horizontal direction and 32 in the vertical direction. Overall max 1984 points. The number of points set by this command should match the number of control points specified using Set Manual Warp Control Points command.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: WarpTableIndex
Param.Desc: Start index in the table for the data to be written

Param.Name: WarpPoints
Param.Desc: Warp map points in X, Y pairs where X, Y are are in 13.3 fixed point format. The range is 13 to 3.

Function.Name: ReadManualWarpTable
Function.Desc: This command reads from the warp map table already loaded using Set Manual Warp Table command. N warp map points (that does not exceed the command packet size) can be read at a time from anywhere within the table. Maximum table size is 1952.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: WarpPoints
Param.Desc: Warp map points in X, Y pairs where X, Y are in 13.3 fixed point format

Param.Name: WarpTableIndex
Param.Desc: Start index in the table from which the data is to be read

Param.Name: NumEntries
Param.Desc: Number of entries to be read

Function.Name: WriteManualWarpControlPoints
Function.Desc: This command sets up the user defined control points of the warp map that shall be applied on top of the keystone correction, anamorphic scaling and other warp dependent feature settings if they are enabled. The warping map table loaded by the manual warp table write command is used as a two dimensional array with dimension which is defined based on the first argument of this command: - TRUE = (NumHorzCtrlPoints x NumVertCtrlPoints) - FALSE = (62 x 32) The points in the map should lie within the display area defined by display image size command. Any points lying outside the display area shall get cropped.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ControlPointsDefinedByArray
Param.Desc: Indicates if the warp control points are explicitly defined by array of horizontal and verical control points. 0 = Input image is uniformally divided to create equally spaced warp control points of dimension (NumHorzCtrlPoints x NumVertCtrlPoints). Warping map table loaded by the Set Manual Warp Table command is used as a two dimensional array with dimension (WarpColumns x WarpRows). 1 = Warp control points are defined by HorizontalCtrlPoints and VerticalCtrlPoints parameters of this command. Warping map table loaded by the Set Manual Warp Table command is used as a two dimensional array with dimension (62 x 32).

Param.Name: HorizontalCtrlPoints
Param.Desc: if ControlPointsDefinedByArray = 0, Send NumHorzCtrlPoints here. if ControlPointsDefinedByArray = 1, Send 62 horizontal control points here in uint16 format

Param.Name: VerticalCtrlPoints
Param.Desc: if ControlPointsDefinedByArray = 0, Send NumVertCtrlPoints here. if ControlPointsDefinedByArray = 1, Send 32 vertical control points here in uint16 format

Function.Name: ReadManualWarpControlPoints
Function.Desc: This command gets up the user defined warping map control points.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ControlPointsDefinedByArray
Param.Desc: Indicates if the warp control points are explicitly defined by array of horizontal and verical control points.

Param.Name: CtrlPoints
Param.Desc: if ControlPointsDefinedByArray = 0, Number of Horizontal control points followed by Number of Vertical control points are returned here if ControlPointsDefinedByArray = 1, Actual(62) Horizontal control points followed by Actual(32) Vertical control points are returned here

Function.Name: WriteKeystoneCorners
Function.Desc: Configures the 2D Keystone correction when the corners of the corrected image are known. Keystone correction is used to remove the distortion caused when the projector is not orthogonal to the projection surface (screen). For the effects to take place, the Keystone feature has to be enabled.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Desc: Returns the keystone configuration parameters currently set. This command should be used when the keystone correction has been configured using the four corners of the corrected image. The keystone correction is observed only if the keystone feature is enabled, even if the parameters are configured correctly.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: WriteEdgeBlendingConfiguration
Function.Desc: This command sets overlap and geometry parameters for semi-manual edge blending, creates and applies blending and warping maps for given blending inputs. It is necessary to call commands to enable manual warping and enable edge blending separately, for the results to take effect. Geometry parameters are dependent on geometric adjustment type. For no geometric correction, no parameters are used. For correction by keystone corners, the 8 parameters are the (x,y) coordinates (zero based) of the keystone corners in raster scan order: top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: OverlapHorizontal
Param.Desc: Horizontal Overlap with other projectors

Param.Name: OverlapVertical
Param.Desc: Vertical Overlap with other projectors

Param.Name: GeometricAdjustmentType
Param.Desc: Geometric Adjustment Type

Param.Name: StorageOptions
Param.Desc: Storage Options

Param.Name: GeometryParams
Param.Desc: Geometric adjustment parameters. Dependent on adjustment type used

Function.Name: ReadEdgeBlendingConfiguration
Function.Desc: This command gets geometry and overlap parameters for semi-manual edge blending. Geometry parameters are dependent on geometric adjustment type. For no geometric correction, no parameters are used. For correction by keystone corners, the 8 parameters are the (x,y) coordinates (zero based) of the keystone corners in raster scan order: top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: OverlapHorizontal
Param.Desc: Horizontal Overlap with other projectors

Param.Name: OverlapVertical
Param.Desc: Vertical Overlap with other projectors

Param.Name: GeometricAdjustmentType
Param.Desc: Geometric Adjustment Type

Param.Name: StorageOptions
Param.Desc: Storage Options

Param.Name: GeometryParams
Param.Desc: Geometric adjustment parameters. Dependent on adjustment type used

Function.Name: WriteImageAlgorithmEnable
Function.Desc: Sets enable flag for all Image Algorithms. 0 = Disable 1 = Enable **Chroma Transient Improvement** : This function enables/disables the Chroma Transient Improvement (CTI) function which filters the 4:4:4 sampled, chrominance (Cr and Cb) data on the B and C data channels. The chroma transient functions performs band pass filtering (supports two center frequencies) and median filtering for ringing minimization. It performs limiting and coring functions for the filtered output. **Gamma Correction** : This function enables/disables the Gamma Correction function which implements the removal of gamma transfer function applied at the source, via table lookup process called de-gamma. When enabled, perform de-gamma translation of the 10-bit RGB input to the common 12-bit floating point (S0M8E4) RGB output. When disabled, the full 10 bits of each data input to the Gamma Correction function are zero padded and MSB-aligned to 12-bits and passed through unmodified. **Color Coordinate Adjustment** : This function enables/disables the Spatially Adaptive Seven Primaries Color Correction Function Enable. When Disable forces 3x3 CSC (Color Space Conversion) with identity. **Brilliant Color** : This function enables/disables the BrilliantColor technology, Brilliant Color uses up to five colors, instead of just the three primary colors, red, green and blue, to improve color accuracy and brightens of secondary colors. This results in a new level of color performance that increases the brightness of the colors. **White Point Correction** : This function enables/disables the White Point Correction, typically used on LED type illumination systems. Sometimes due to increase in LED operating temperature or LED aging the LEDs output wavelentgh drifts, therefore white point of the system shifts. This algorithm using active light sensor feedback and factory calibrated values help maintaing white point of the system. **Dynamic Black** : Dynamic Black (DB) is an algorithm that reduces the amount of light reaching the projection path by means of LED output power through current control and compensates for reduced light by gaining up the RGB signals. **HDR** : High Dynamic Range (HDR) is an algorithm that maps wider brightness and color range of HDR source to the projector display range. HDR is affected by several factors such as illimuniation characterstics, duty cycle distribution and current running sequence. A valid HDR source should be set by HDR_SetHdrSourceConfiguration() before enabling HDR processing. Note: **Chroma Transient Improvement** is applicable to Analog SDTV sources only. DLPC654x controller doesn't support Analog sources. Even if enabled on DLPC65x controller, there is no changes in the displayed image when enabled.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: ReadImageAlgorithmEnable
Function.Desc: Returns enable flag for all Image Algorithms '0' - Disabled or algorithm feature not available. '1' - Enabled

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: WriteImageBrightness
Function.Desc: The brightness control provides the ability to add or subtract a fixed bias from each of the input channels. This may be used to remove any inherent offsets and/or adjust the brightness level. The brightness coefficients are signed, 11-bit (s8.2), 2's complement values between -256 and 255.75, inclusive. Brightness Control is used after color space conversion.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: BrightnessAdjustment
Param.Desc: Brightness Adjustment. The range is -256 to 255.75.

Function.Name: ReadImageBrightness
Function.Desc: Returns Image Brightness Level.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: BrightnessAdjustment
Param.Desc: Brightness Adjustment. The range is -256 to 255.75.

Function.Name: WriteImageContrast
Function.Desc: Sets Image Contrast in percentage. Each contrast byte controls the gain applied to the input image data for a given data channel. The contrast gain has a range from 0 to 200 (0% to 200%) with 100 (100%) being nominal (default).

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Contrast
Param.Desc: Contrast. The range is 0 to 200.

Function.Name: ReadImageContrast
Function.Desc: Returns Image Contrast in percentage.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Contrast
Param.Desc: Contrast. The range is 0 to 200.

Param.Name: HueAdjustmentAngle
Param.Desc: Hue Adjustment Angle. The range is -45 to 45.

Param.Name: ColorControlGain
Param.Desc: Color Control Gain. The range is 0 to 200.

Function.Name: ReadImageHueAndColorControl
Function.Desc: Returns Image Hue Adjustment angle in degrees and Color Control Gain in percentage.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: HueAdjustmentAngle
Param.Desc: Hue Adjustment Angle. The range is -45 to 45.

Param.Name: ColorControlGain
Param.Desc: Color Control Gain. The range is 0 to 200.

Function.Name: WriteImageSharpness
Function.Desc: Configures the sharpness filter. A value of 0 is the least sharp (smoothest), while a value of 31 is the sharpest. This filter is in the back end of the data path, so both video and graphics are affected. TI recommends that the sharpness filters be disabled (sharpness=16) for graphics sources.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Sharpness
Param.Desc: Sharpness value to apply.The range is 0 to 31.

Function.Name: ReadImageSharpness
Function.Desc: Returns the current sharpness value

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Sharpness
Param.Desc: Sharpness value to apply.The range is 0 to 31.

Function.Name: WriteImageRgbOffset
Function.Desc: Offsets the levels of the RGB channels at a point in the data path after the following image processing functions have been applied - source offset, contrast, RGB Gain, brightness and color space conversion (including hue and color adjustment).

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: RedOffset
Param.Desc: Red channel offset setting.The range is -256 to 255.75.

Param.Name: GreenOffset
Param.Desc: Green channel offset setting.The range is -256 to 255.75.

Param.Name: BlueOffset
Param.Desc: Blue channel offset setting.The range is -256 to 255.75.

Function.Desc: Returns Red, Green and Blue channel offset settings.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: RedOffset
Param.Desc: Red channel offset setting.The range is -256 to 255.75.

Param.Name: GreenOffset
Param.Desc: Green channel offset setting.The range is -256 to 255.75.

Param.Name: BlueOffset
Param.Desc: Blue channel offset setting.The range is -256 to 255.75.

Function.Name: WriteImageRgbGain
Function.Desc: Adjusts individual R, G and B gains of the source image. Gain is specified as a percentage from 0% - 200%, with 100% being nominal (no gain change). 0% will zero out the channel. This function adjusts R, G and B gains by altering the Color Space Converter (CSC) coefficients. This function is only applicable to RGB sources.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: RedGain
Param.Desc: Red channel gain setting.The range is 0 to 200.

Param.Name: GreenGain
Param.Desc: Green channel gain setting.The range is 0 to 200.

Param.Name: BlueGain
Param.Desc: Blue channel gain setting.The range is 0 to 200.

Function.Name: ReadImageRgbGain
Function.Desc: Returns gain setting for Red, Green and Blue color channels in percentage.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: RedGain
Param.Desc: Red channel gain setting.The range is 0 to 200.

Param.Name: GreenGain
Param.Desc: Green channel gain setting.The range is 0 to 200.

Param.Name: BlueGain
Param.Desc: Blue channel gain setting.The range is 0 to 200.

Function.Name: WriteCscTable
Function.Desc: Sets the Color Space Conversion Matrix with one of the CSC tables stored in flash.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Index
Param.Desc: Index of the pre-defined CSC table in flash.The range is 0 to 7.

Function.Name: ReadCscTable
Function.Desc: Gets the index of the Color Space Conversion Matrix that is currently cofigured for use.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Index
Param.Desc: Index of the pre-defined CSC table.A value of SRC_CSC_MAXTABLE indicates that no table(from the flash) is being used.

Function.Name: WriteImageCcaCoordinates
Function.Desc: This Command allows independent adjustment of the primary, secondary and white coordinates. This call will override any CCA settings performed by prior calls.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: ReadImageCcaCoordinates
Function.Desc: Returns the current color coordinate configuration.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: WriteImageHsg
Function.Desc: This command applies the given hue, saturation and gain values for all colors. It does not affect colors having a gain of zero. Note: This call will override any CCA settings performed by prior calls.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: ReadImageHsg
Function.Desc: This command returns the currently applied hue, saturation and gain values for all the colors. If gain for a color is zero then the HSG is not applied on the color.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: WriteImageGammaLut
Function.Desc: This command loads the specified gamma look-up table into memory from flash. A single load is accomplished by loading data for red, green and blue look-up tables. Use DLP Composer(tm) to create new gamma tables or modify existing gamma tables.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: GammaLutNumber
Param.Desc: Gamma look-up table to load.

Function.Name: ReadImageGammaLut
Function.Desc: Returns the table number of the gamma look-up table currently loaded in memory.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: GammaLutNumber
Param.Desc: Gamma look-up table to load.

Function.Name: WriteImageGammaCurveShift
Function.Desc: Used to specify the shifts in the gamma curve of Red, Green and Blue. A left shift is a positive offset and a right shift is a negative offset. The effective brightness is increased with a left shift and decreased with a right shift.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: RedShift
Param.Desc: Red gamma curve shift.The range is -128 to 127.

Param.Name: GreenShift
Param.Desc: Green gamma curve shift.The range is -128 to 127.

Param.Name: BlueShift
Param.Desc: Blue gamma curve shift.The range is -128 to 127.

Param.Name: AllColorShift
Param.Desc: Broadcasted shift to gamma curves of all color.The range is -128 to 127.

Function.Name: ReadImageGammaCurveShift
Function.Desc: Returns Image Gamma Shift for red, green and blue as well as shift to be broadcasted to all colors

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: RedShift
Param.Desc: Red gamma curve shift.The range is -128 to 127.

Param.Name: GreenShift
Param.Desc: Green gamma curve shift.The range is -128 to 127.

Param.Name: BlueShift
Param.Desc: Blue gamma curve shift.The range is -128 to 127.

Param.Name: AllColorShift
Param.Desc: Broadcasted shift to gamma curves of all color.The range is -128 to 127.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: WhitePeakingVal
Param.Desc: Amount of white processing. Range 0 to MaxValue (set by IMG_SetWhitePeakingRange()). The default value of MaxValue is 10.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: WhitePeakingVal
Param.Desc: Amount of white processing. Range 0 to MaxValue (set by IMG_SetWhitePeakingRange()). The default value of MaxValue is 10.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Strength
Param.Desc: Filter Strength setting determines how much of high frequency content is filtered out. Valid range 0-7Setting of 0 means least filtering of high frequency content (sharpest image; more flicker) Setting of 7 means most filtering of high frequency content (smoothest image; least flicker)

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Strength
Param.Desc: Filter Strength setting determines how much of high frequency content is filtered out. Valid range 0-7Setting of 0 means least filtering of high frequency content (sharpest image; more flicker) Setting of 7 means most filtering of high frequency content (smoothest image; least flicker)

Function.Name: WriteHdrSourceConfiguration
Function.Desc: HDR maps wider brightness and color range of HDR sources to projector brightness and color range. The mapping requires multiple source groups and system groups define the HDR source and projection device properties respectively. This command sets the source properties and based on this information nearest source group is selected for mapping.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: ReadHdrSourceConfiguration
Function.Desc: Includes the metadata information.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: WriteHdrStrengthSetting
Function.Desc: Sets HDR strength which adjusts the electro-optical transfer function that is applied on the input HDR video signal. HDR strength can vary with the ambient brightness level.HDR strength is not applicable for HLG transfer function set by HDR source configuration.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: HdrStrength
Param.Desc: HDR Strength. The range is 0 to 10.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: HdrStrength
Param.Desc: HDR Strength. The range is 0 to 10.

Function.Name: WriteSystemBrightnessRangeSetting
Function.Desc: Sets the system brightness range in nits. These are used in determining the appropriate EOTF and OOTF function to be applied on the HDR source. This need to set only for HDR functionality.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: MinBrightness
Param.Desc: Min Brightness. The range is 0 to 10000.

Param.Name: MaxBrightness
Param.Desc: Max Brightness. The range is 0 to 10000.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: MinBrightness
Param.Desc: Min Brightness. The range is 0 to 10000.

Param.Name: MaxBrightness
Param.Desc: Max Brightness. The range is 0 to 10000.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ColorProfile
Param.Desc: Color Profile

Function.Name: WriteImagePointHsg
Function.Desc: This command applies the given hue, saturation and gain values for all colors, for a specified sample point. Point is a number 0-15 corresponding to one of the the 5 x 3 PCC sample points in raster scan order. It does not affect colors having a gain of zero. Note: This call will override any CCA settings performed by prior calls.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Point
Param.Desc: Point

Function.Name: ReadImagePointHsg
Function.Desc: This command returns the currently applied hue, saturation and gain values for all the colors, for a specified sample point. Point is a number 0-15 corresponding to one of the the 5 x 3 PCC sample points in raster scan order. If gain for a color is zero then the HSG is not applied on the color.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Point
Param.Desc: Point

Function.Name: WriteSpccControlPoints
Function.Desc: Sets positions of control points for Multipoint sPCC.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ControlPointY
Param.Desc: Sets vertical position for (row, col) sample points (1,0), (1,1), (1,2), (1,3), (1,4)

Param.Name: ControlPointX1
Param.Desc: Sets horizontal position for (row, col) sample points (0,1), (1,1), (2,1)

Param.Name: ControlPointX2
Param.Desc: Sets horizontal position for (row, col) sample points (0,3), (1,3), (2,3)

Function.Name: ReadSpccControlPoints
Function.Desc: Returns positions of control points for Multipoint sPCC

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ControlPointY
Param.Desc: Gets vertical position for sample points (1,0), (1,1), (1,2), (1,3), (1,4)

Param.Name: ControlPointX1
Param.Desc: Gets horizontal position for (row, col) sample points (0,1), (1,1), (2,1)

Param.Name: ControlPointX2
Param.Desc: Gets horizontal position for (row, col) sample points (0,3), (1,3), (2,3)

Function.Name: WritePccCoefficientsDirect
Function.Desc: This command applies raw PCC Coefficients for all colors through direct access, for a specified sample point. Point is a number 0-15 corresponding to one of the the 5 x 3 PCC sample points in raster scan order. Note: This call will override any CCA settings performed by prior calls.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Point
Param.Desc: Point

Function.Name: ReadPccCoefficientsDirect

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Point
Param.Desc: Point

Function.Name: ReadDmdTemperature
Function.Desc: This command applicable only if TMP411A temperature sensor is installed in the system.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Temperature
Param.Desc: value in degree CelciusNote: As a default condition, the firmware is configured to read TMP411A outputs using I2C port 2. This can be changed as desired. Using latest DLP Composer SW, the firmware can be rebuilt to receive serial data via I2C at ports 1 and 2 to which the hardware would need to be configured.The range is -256 to 255.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: FanNum
Param.Desc: fan number whose duty cycle is to be modified Range 1-3

Param.Name: SwFreq
Param.Desc: Switching frequency of the fan SFREQ_100KHZ 100khz SFREQ_24HZ 24hz

Param.Name: DutyCycle
Param.Desc: New duty cycle of the fan Range 0 and 30 - 100 in steps of 5. For PMD with version lesser than 0x11 the max duty cycle is restricted to 95%.

Param.Name: Select
Param.Desc: Select the 0 = PMD 0 1 = PMD 1 2 = PMD 2

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: SwFreq

Param.Name: Select

Function.Name: WriteEepromLockState
Function.Desc: Sets the lock state of EEPROM. When lock is set, all writes to EEPROM settings and/or calibration data from application software will not be actually written to the EEPROM. The locked mode is to be used only in factory where user wants to play around with various settings without actually recording them in the EEPROM. In normal use mode, the lock is not supposed to be set.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: LockState
Param.Desc: '0' - Unlocked '1' - Locked

Function.Name: ReadEepromLockState
Function.Desc: Gets the lock state of EEPROM.

Param.Name: Summary

Param.Desc: '0' - Unlocked '1' - Locked

Function.Name: WriteIlluminationEnable
Function.Desc: Enables or Disables the illumination system. Additionally, For Systems with color wheel: - Illumination will be turned on only if the system has indicated that the color wheel is spinning This interlock is necessary to avoid burning the coatings off the glass For Lamp Based Systems with Lamp Strike Reset Enabled: - Only Illumination Disable is supported during run time To Enable illumination again, system should be powered down and then powered up again This is necessary to protect DMD from EMI generated during lamp strike event

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: 0 - Disabled 1 - Only Red LED Enabled 2 - Only Green LED Enabled 3 - Red and Green LEDs Enabled 4 - Only Blue LED Enabled 5 - Red and Blue LEDs Enabled 6 - Green and Blue LEDs Enabled 7 - All LEDs Enabled

Function.Name: WriteDlpa3005IlluminationCurrent
Function.Desc: Sets DLPA3005 Drive Current Levels input as 10-bit DriveLevel per LED in the range 0 - 874. Command should not be used if Dynamic Black or White Point Correction is enabled Current output in Amps is calculated as described below. OutputCurrent = ((DriveLevel + 1)/1024)*((0.15/0.004)) Amps Example: For DriveLevel = 874; OutputCurrent = 32.04345703Amps

Function.Name: WriteCwIndexDelay
Function.Desc: This command is used to time delay the passing of the color wheel index signal inside the ASIC. If the color wheel index generation is accomplished with a stripe on the hub of the motor, the relationship of the stripe to the first color may be skewed. This command can be used to attempt to correct this skew by repositioning the index electronically.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Wheel
Param.Desc: CW select. CW_1ST - 0x00: First Color Wheel CW_2ND - 0x01: Second Color Wheel CW_3RD - 0x02: Third Color Wheel

Param.Name: DegreesInteger
Param.Desc: Index delay value in degrees Integer part. Range 0 - 359

Param.Name: DegreesFraction
Param.Desc: Index delay value in degrees Fraction part. Range 0 -9

Function.Name: ReadCwIndexDelay
Function.Desc: Gets the time delay of the skewed colorwheel index

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: DegreesInteger
Param.Desc: Index delay value in degress Integer part Range 0 - 359

Param.Name: DegreesFraction
Param.Desc: Index delay value in degrees Fraction part Range 0 -9

Param.Name: Wheel
Param.Desc: CW select.CW_1ST - 0x00: First Color Wheel CW_2ND - 0x01: Second Color Wheel CW_3RD - 0x02: Third Color Wheel CW_ALL - 0x03: combination of CW_1ST , CW_2ND , CW_3RD

Function.Name: WriteInitializeOnTheFlyLoadSplashImage
Function.Desc: This Command Initializes the on the fly splash load operation. It is used to give the information about the image (dimensions, pixel format, compression used and so on) and sets the data path for the splash load operation. It also resets prior operations. It should be called once before loading an image on the fly.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: SplashHeader
Param.Desc: Header of the pattern Image (same format as the splash header)

Param.Name: Width
Param.Desc: Width of the splash image, in pixels, to which given pattern is to be replicated

Param.Name: Height
Param.Desc: Height of the splash image, in pixels, to which given pattern is to be replicated

Function.Name: WriteLoadSplashImageOnTheFly
Function.Desc: This Command receives the image data and writes it into the VPS DRAM Buffer. It performs decompression if required. Due to the limitation of USB, the entire image data may not be sent in one go. Hence, this command has to be called multiple times in order to load the entire image. It also stores any bytes that are unprocessed by the API (if the API does not have all the bytes required to perform an operation). These unprocessed bytes are sent over to callback function the next time data is received. The API displays the loaded splash once the entire image is loaded into the memory. The remaining pixels to load the image is also returned by the callback function.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ImageData
Param.Desc: Image Data

Function.Name: WriteXprCalibrationPatternDisplay
Function.Desc: This command loads a pre-defined XPR Calibration pattern as a splash image and displays it on the screen. A 64x64 pattern is repeated over a 3840x2160 display area.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: WriteXpr4WayOrientation
Function.Desc: This command sets the orientation number of the actuator position (which gets stored in EEPROM) There are 24 possible options 0 - 23; use this commmand while performing XPR calibration using TI provided XPR calibration splash image. Note: Use Display Image Size command to make sure the display area is 3840x2160 If the reported display resolution is <= 1080p this command will not have any influence on the displayed image.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: XprOrientationNumber
Param.Desc: Orientation number. Range 0 - 23.

Function.Name: ReadXpr4WayOrientation
Function.Desc: This command retrieves the last set orientation number or the subframe order

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: XprOrientationNumber
Param.Desc: Refer software for XPR_Orientation_Number definitions.

Function.Name: WriteXprActuatorWaveformControlParameter
Function.Desc:
This command configures/sets up the Actuator Waveform Control(AWC) block. Here, AWCx can be AWC 0 or 1. Bytes 2-5 contains the XPR command data as mentioned in Byte 0. Byte 1 contains AWC channel number, possible values are 0 or 1.
**Fixed Output Enable**: Configures Actuator in fixed output mode. Byte 2: 0x00 - Disable 0x01 - Enable Bytes 3-5: Reserved must be set to 0x000000
**Gain**: Set Waveform Generator DAC/PWM Gain. Byte 2: Range 0 - 255 format u1.7 (0 to 1.9921875) Bytes 3-5: Reserved must be set to 0x000000
**Subframe delay**: Subframe delay Bytes 2-5; Range 0 - 262143 and lsb = 133.333ns
**Actuator Type (READ ONLY)**: Actuator type Byte 2: 0x00 - NONE 0x01 - Optotune (XPR-25 Model) 0x80 - TI Actuator Interface (EEPROM) 0x81 - TI Actuator Interface (MCU) Bytes 3-5: Reserved must be set to 0x000000
**Output Enable/Disable**: Actuator output enable/disable Byte 2: 0x00 - Disable 0x01 - Enable Bytes 3-5: Reserved must be set to 0x000000
Note: Both AWC0 and AWC1 disabled/enabled together **Clock Width**: Defines the high and low width for the output clock (the clock period will be 2*(ClkWidth+1)) 0 = 1 (Clock period is two clocks); lsb = 8.33ns Bytes 2-5: ClkWidth Example: ClkWidth = 0; will generate clock of 2*(0+1)*8.33 = 16.66ns
**Offset**: DAC/PWM Output Offset Byte 2: Range -128 - +127 format S7 (-128 to +127) Bytes 3-5: Reserved must be set to 0x000000
**Number of Segments**: Defines number of segments Byte 2: Range 2 - 255 Bytes 3-5: Reserved must be set to 0x000000
**Segments Length**: Defines size of the segments Bytes 2-3: Range 19 - 4095 Bytes 4-5: Reserved must be set to 0x0000
**Invert PWM A**: Applicable when AWC is configured to PWM type instead of DAC Byte 2: 0x00 - No inversion 0x01 - Inverted Bytes 3-5: Reserved must be set to 0x000000
**Invert PWM B**: Applicable when AWC is configured to PWM type instead of DAC Byte 2: 0x00 - No inversion 0x01 - Inverted Bytes 3-5: Reserved must be set to 0x000000
**Subframe Filter Value**: Sets Subframe Filter Value - defines the minimum time between Subframe edges. Edges closer than the set value will be filtered out Byte 2: 0 = Filter disabled, >0 = Filter time will be Val x 60us, Range: 0 - 255 Bytes 3-5: Reserved must be set to 0x000000
**Subframe Watch Dog**: Defines the maximum time between Subframe edges; if timer expires, then the WG will automatically output the Fixed Output value, and the normal output will resume on the next subframe edge. Bytes 2-3: 0 = Subframe watchdog disabled, >0 = Watchdog time will be Time x 60us, Range: Range: 0 - 1023 Bytes 4-5: Reserved must be set to 0x0000
**Fixed Output Value** : Defines the value to be output on DAC/PWM when fixed output mode is selected. Byte 2: Value to be output on DAC/PWM, Range -128 to 127 Bytes 3-5: Reserved must be set to 0x000000
**Note**: To use **Subframe Filter Value** and **Subframe Watch Dog** care must be taken to set a value which aproximately 10% more than 2x of the operating frequency. For example - 4K @ 60Hz, the value can be set as (1/(60*2))*1.10*10^6 = 9166us.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: XprCommand
Param.Desc: XPR Command

Param.Name: AwcChannel
Param.Desc: Channel number (0 or 1) of Actuator waveform control for which the command parameter has to be applied

Param.Desc: Data that needs to be passed to the command

Function.Name: WriteLoad4WayXprWaveform
Function.Desc: This command sets the waveform of the AWC channel with the data and index value passed Format of data that needs to be passed: FallingEdgeCoil1 FallingEdgeCoil2 for first 256 Index values RisingEdgeCoil1 RisingEdgeCoil2 for subsequent 256 Index values

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: AwcChannel
Param.Desc: Channel number of AWC for which the command parameter has to be applied

Param.Name: Index
Param.Desc: Index from which the data passed need to be updated to the waveform

Param.Name: Data
Param.Desc: Data that needs to be set to the waveform.

Function.Name: WriteDbAperturePosition
Function.Desc: This command controls how far open or closed the aperture should be over the light source. The aperture position is adjusted every frame based on the current scene brightness. Note: This function should only be used during calibration and only when the DynamicBlack(tm) II algorithm is disabled.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Position
Param.Desc: Setting to which aperture should be positioned. Range = 1 to (Composer configured number of aperture positions).Position = 0 - Aperture is fully closed Position = 0xFFFF - Aperture is fully open

Function.Name: ReadDbAperturePosition
Function.Desc: This command gives how far open or closed the aperture is over the light source. The aperture position is adjusted every frame based on the current scene brightness. Note: This function should only be used during calibration and only when the DynamicBlack algorithm is disabled.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Position
Param.Desc: Position

Function.Name: WriteDbApertureMinMax
Function.Desc: This command controls the minimum and maximum aperture positions. The minimum command typically corresponds to the aperture position for the aperture to be fully closed, while the maximum command typically corresponds to the aperture position for the aperture to be fully open. However, the polarity of these may change depending on the aperture hardware used.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: AptMin
Param.Desc: Position where the aperture is fully open or closed (depending on aperture hardware polarity). Range = 0 to (Composer configured minimum), and must be less than Apt_Max.

Param.Name: AptMax
Param.Desc: Position where the aperture is fully open or closed (depending on aperture hardware polarity). Range = 0 to (Composer configured maximum), and must be greater than Apt_Min.

Function.Name: ReadDbApertureMinMax
Function.Desc: This command gets the minimum and maximum aperture positions. The minimum command typically corresponds to the aperture position for the aperture to be fully closed, while the maximum command typically corresponds to the aperture position for the aperture to be fully open. However, the polarity of these may change depending on the aperture hardware used.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: AptMin
Param.Desc: Position where the aperture is fully open or closed (depending on aperture hardware polarity).

Param.Name: AptMax
Param.Desc: Position where the aperture is fully open or closed (depending on aperture hardware polarity).

Function.Name: WriteDbManualMode
Function.Desc: This command enables the DynamicBlack Manual Mode of operation. It is intended to be used as a test command. The DB software algorithm will be disabled, and the user can set manual gain values using DB set gain command.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: 1 = enable manual mode 0 = disable manual mode

Function.Name: ReadDbManualMode
Function.Desc: This command enables the DynamicBlack Manual Mode of operation. It is intended to be used as a test command. The DB software algorithm will be disabled, and the user can set manual gain values using DB set gain command.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: 1 = enable manual mode 0 = disable manual mode

Function.Name: WriteDbBorderConfiguration
Function.Desc: This command configures area of the DynamicBlack border region for the border exclusion function. The border exclusion function allows the user to reduce the letterbox (black border) effect on a primarily bright image where letterbox area reduces the overall scene brightness for the algorithm. It also helps the algorithm better handle images with bright subtitles where the subtitles increase the overall scene brightness. This command will also be used in a multi-controller configuration to exclude any image overlap required for other image processing algorithms.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Top
Param.Desc: number of lines top of border. Range 0 - 4095

Param.Name: Bottom
Param.Desc: number of lines bottom of border. Range 0 - 4095

Param.Name: Left
Param.Desc: number of pixels of left border. Range 0 - 4095

Param.Name: Right
Param.Desc: number of pixels of right border. Range 0 - 4095

Function.Name: ReadDbBorderConfiguration
Function.Desc: This Command returns the border region area for the DynamicBlack border exclusion function.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Top
Param.Desc: number of lines top of border. Range 0 - 4095

Param.Name: Bottom
Param.Desc: number of lines bottom of border. Range 0 - 4095

Param.Name: Left
Param.Desc: number of pixels of left border. Range 0 - 4095

Param.Name: Right
Param.Desc: number of pixels of right border. Range 0 - 4095

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: BorderWeight
Param.Desc: Weight value of border pixels 0 = 0% weighted; 1 = 25% weighted; 2 = 50% weighted; 3 = 75% weighted

Function.Name: ReadDbBorderWeight
Function.Desc: Sets weight value of the DynamicBlack border region for the border exclusion function

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: BorderWeight
Param.Desc: Weight value of border pixels 0 = 0% weighted; 1 = 25% weighted; 2 = 50% weighted; 3 = 75% weighted

Function.Name: WriteDbClipPixels

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ClipPixels
Param.Desc: Number of pixels that can be clipped. Range = 0 to 65535.

Function.Name: ReadDbClipPixels
Function.Desc: This command returns the currently selected number of pixels that can be clipped.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: ClipPixels
Param.Desc: Clip Pixels

Function.Name: WriteDbGain
Function.Desc: This command controls the DynamicBlack gain value. Typical value range is 1.0 to 8.0. Manual Mode needs to be enabled to set the gain as it will override the gain value that is calculated every frame.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: DbGain
Param.Desc: Gain value. Typical value range is 1.0 to 8.0.

Function.Name: ReadDbGain
Function.Desc: This command gets the DynamicBlack gain value. Typical value range is 1.0 to 8.0

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: DbGain
Param.Desc: Gain value. Typical value range is 1.0 to 8.0.

Function.Name: WriteDbNumSteps
Function.Desc: This command sets the number of steps to allow the DynamicBlack aperture to move. This allows the number of steps to be adjusted dynamically so that the results can be visually compared.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Steps
Param.Desc: Number of steps to allow aperture to move. Range = 1 to (Composer configured number of aperture positions).

Function.Name: ReadDbNumSteps
Function.Desc: This command returns currently configured number of steps to allow the DynamicBlack aperture to move.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Steps
Param.Desc: Steps

Function.Name: WriteDbApertureSpeed
Function.Desc: This command specifies the number of positions that the DynamicBlack aperture can move in one frame.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Speed
Param.Desc: Number of positions that the aperture can move in one frame. Range = 1 to 256.

Function.Name: ReadDbApertureSpeed
Function.Desc: This command returns the number of positions that the DynamicBlack aperture can move in one frame.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Speed
Param.Desc: Speed

Function.Name: WriteDbStrength
Function.Desc: This function controls the DynamicBlack strength. A higher strength value will cause DynamicBlack(tm) II to be more aggressive in closing the aperture for a given scene brightness. The aperture min and max positions have to be set prior to calling this function. The aperture movement strength range is 0 to 3, with a recommended value of 2.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Strength
Param.Desc: Aperture movement strength setting. Range = 0 to 3, with a recommended value of 2.

Function.Name: ReadDbStrength
Function.Desc: This function returns the DynamicBlack strength setting.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Strength
Param.Desc: Strength

Function.Name: ReadDbHistogram
Function.Desc: This command returns the start address of the DynamicBlack(DB) histogram data. The histogram contains scene brightness data from the previous frame. The DB histogram contains 34 bins measuring non-overlapping intensity ranges in the displayed image. The value of each bin equals the number of pixels within the bin's intensity range. Each pixel's intensity is calculated as the maximum of its red, green, and blue values. In other words, pixel intensity = MAX( R, G, B ). Each pixel has a format of unsigned 8.8, making 16 bit values. Bins 32 and 33 are special bins that represent pixels that have values of exactly zero and only fractional values respectively. This function can be used independently of aperture control for image improvement in dark scenes.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: HistPtr
Param.Desc: Start address of the DB histogram array. Array size is 34. The LSB of each bin represents 32 pixels. Each bin saturates at 0x0003FFFF.

Function.Name: ReadDbCurrentApertPos
Function.Desc: This function returns the current aperture position. This position indicates how far open or closed the aperture is over the light source. The aperture position is adjusted every frame based on the current scene brightness.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Position
Param.Desc: Position

Function.Name: WriteEnableXprCalibrationMode
Function.Desc: This command sets the system in bypass mode. Setting the system in bypass mode disables any image processing to establish one to one correspondence between pixels on input source image and display image. Desirable for seeing clear splits of XPR subframes. There is no exit from calibration mode. Please restart the system.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: 1 - Calibration mode enabled

Function.Name: ReadEnableXprCalibrationMode
Function.Desc: This command gets the state of XPR calibration mode. Whether enabled or not.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: 0 - Calibration mode not enabled1 - Calibration mode enabled

Function.Name: WriteWpcTargetManualMode
Function.Desc: Sets/Resets the manual mode for speciying WPC target color point at run-time. When manual mode is set, all target color points specified in the project will be ignored. Software will set only the user specified target color point until the manual mode is reset using this same command.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: Manual Mode Enable/Disable

Function.Name: ReadWpcTargetManualMode
Function.Desc: Gets whether the manual mode for speciying WPC target color point at run-time is active. When manual mode is set, all target color points specified in the project will be ignored. Software will set only the user specified target color point until the manual mode is reset.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: Manual Mode Enabled/Disabled

Function.Name: WriteDebugMessageMask
Function.Desc: Set enable mask for debug messages. The mask identifies the sources of debug messages which are to be enabled for printing at the UART debug port. The mask bit corresponding to the source has to be set to enable it.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: ReadDebugMessageMask
Function.Desc: Retrieves the current debug message mask. The mask decides which sources of debug messages are enabled. A value of 1 in the mask bit corresponding to a source means that the source is enabled.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: WriteEnableUsbDebugLog
Function.Desc: Enables or disables the USB logging of messages. When USB logging is enabled, UART logging is stopped.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enable
Param.Desc: 1 = Enable debug log on USB port 0 = Disable debug log on USB port

Function.Name: WriteEepromMemory
Function.Desc: Writes data to EEPROM connected to the controller. The EEPROM holds settings and calibration data. The primary purpose of this function is for the user to write to areas of EEPROM outside of the settings and calibration data. If user wants to overwrite settings/calibration data, the password sent with the command should match the expected password. This is a protection mechanism to prevent accidental overwrite of settings/calibration data.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Index
Param.Desc: Index of the memory to write (0 means first byte in EEPROM, 1 means second byte and so on)

Param.Name: Size
Param.Desc: Number of bytes to be written

Param.Name: Pwd
Param.Desc: EEPROM password if the user needs to write to the TI specified memory space

Param.Name: Data
Param.Desc: Data

Function.Name: ReadEepromMemory
Function.Desc: This function reads data from EEPROM connected to the Controller which has settings and calibration data. Note: EEPROM data can be read without unlocking.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Data
Param.Desc: Data

Param.Name: Index
Param.Desc: Index of the memory to read from

Param.Name: Size
Param.Desc: Number of bytes to read

Function.Name: WriteTiActuatorInterfaceDebug
Function.Desc: Command used to query actuator related information for debugging purpose. Use this command to retrieve information when actuator not running or system is in standby state.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: TiActQueryType
Param.Desc: Query type 0 = Query N number of bytes from offset address provided in next two bytes i.e., Bytes 1-2 1 = Query Actuator information also print on UART debug port 2 = Query AWG Data Set for index number provided in next two bytes i.e., Bytes 1-2 3 = Query AWG Edge table header for index number in next two bytes i.e., Bytes 1-2

Param.Name: TiActAddr
Param.Desc: Query type provided in Byte 0; not applicable when Query type = 1

Param.Name: TiActNumData
Param.Desc: Number of bytes to be read when Query type = 0. (Note: Maximum 32 bytes can be read at a time.)

Function.Name: WriteCwDualTrackMode
Function.Desc: This function sets the strength, or speed, at which the secondary color wheel will track the primary color wheel with respect to phase alignment. This function has no affect if there is only one color wheel in the system. It is recommended to start the system with either @ref CW_TRACK_NORMAL or @ref CW_TRACK_SLOW, then increase the strength as needed. @ref CW_TRACK_FAST should be used with caution as some wheels may become unstable with this setting.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Mode
Param.Desc: @ref CW_TRACK_SLOW, @ref CW_TRACK_NORMAL, @ref CW_TRACK_FAST ;default is CW_TRACK_NORMAL

Function.Name: ReadLibSignature
Function.Desc: Command Handler for reading the signature for a single library. The command expects one byte of data which is the library index. It then returns the library name and changeset information as two null terminated strings. The maximum length of the library name is CS_LIB_NAME_MAX_LEN characters. The maximum length of the changeset string is CS_CHANGESET_MAX_LEN characters. Any data greater than these limits * will be truncated. Any data lesser than these limits will be padded with null characters to the maximum length. The strings returned will be null terminated.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: LibSignature
Param.Desc: Library Signature

Param.Name: ChangesetSignature
Param.Desc: Changeset Signature

Param.Name: LibName
Param.Desc: @CMD_LibName_t Library Name

Function.Name: WriteTpgRampHorizontal
Function.Desc: Sets the test pattern generator to produce a horizontal ramp with the specified step size. Only R/G/B/Y/C/M/W colors are Supported. For others, Use Color Ramp The ramp starts with intensity zero, depending on step speed reaches the maximum intensity if possible. If full intensity is acheived, the remaining pixels are white To start with some other intensity, use Fixed Ramp

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: RampStep
Param.Desc: Horizontal Ramp step increment

Param.Name: RampColor
Param.Desc: R/G/B/Y/C/M/W color

Function.Name: WriteEnableKeystone
Function.Desc: Enables or disables the keystone correction feature. Keystone correction is required because of the distortion caused when the projector is not orthogonal to the screen. The keystone correction has to be correctly configured using CMD_SetKeystoneAngles() or CMD_SetKeystoneCorners(). The keystone feature has to be enabled once during the system operation for any keystone correction to be applied.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enabled
Param.Desc: Enabled

Function.Name: ReadEnableKeystone
Function.Desc: Informs whether the keystone correction feature is currently enabled or disabled. Keystone correction is applied to remove distortion caused when the projector is not orthogonal to the screen. If the feature is enabled, it means that the keystone correction feature is currently applied.

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Param.Name: Enabled
Param.Desc: Enabled
