Function.Name: WriteUserInterpolationStrengthEnable
Function.Desc: Create the user defined nterpolation strength LUT. This table is composed of 16 entries that are indexed by a 4-bit value representing the motion detected in the input source. The table entries are 5-bit values (maxium supported value is 16) representing the interpolation strength. These values describe a linear transfer function that determines a combination of previous field pixels and newly created interpolated field pixels based on motion intensity values

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Function.Name: ReadUserInterpolationStrengthEnable
Function.Desc: Returns the members of the user defined interpolation strength table

Param.Name: Summary
Param.Desc: Status and summary of command execution.

Member.Name: Stopped
Member.Desc: AutoLock function has never been started or has been commanded to stop.

Member.Name: Autolocked
Member.Desc: AutoLock function has locked to the source.

Member.Name: LockFailed
Member.Desc: AutoLock function is unable to lock.

Member.Name: SourceChangeDetected
Member.Desc: Generated when new sync signals are detected.

Member.Name: SourceDetectedStable
Member.Desc: Generated when sync signals are considered stable.

Member.Name: SourceNoSyncs
Member.Desc: Generated when no syncs are detected.

Member.Name: SourceNoVsyncs
Member.Desc: Generated when no VSYNC is detected.

Member.Name: SourceNoHsyncs
Member.Desc: Generated when no HSYNC is detected.

Member.Name: AsmLocked
Member.Desc: Generated when the algorithm declares the source locked.

Member.Name: DsmLocked
Member.Desc: Generated when the algorithm declares the source locked.

Member.Name: Alf
Member.Desc: Always run Autolock. Never restore from Saved Mode Table.

Member.Name: Smt1Smt2
Member.Desc: Always restore from Saved Mode Table. If no mathcing entries are found, then run Autolock.

Member.Name: AlfSmt1Smt2
Member.Desc: Run Autolock first. On subsequent resyncs, restore from Saved Mode Table.

Member.Name: ToAppDirect
Member.Desc: Switch to application regardless of the BOOT HOLD GPIO State. This option is provided for debug purposes only

Member.Name: ToAppTrueGlobalDirect
Member.Desc: Switch to application with DMD True Global Enabled regardless of the BOOT HOLD GPIO State.

Member.Name: InvalidController
Member.Desc: Invalid Controller for the product configuration

Member.Name: InvalidDmd
Member.Desc: Invalid DMD for the product configuration

Member.Name: InvalidDmdProjectdata
Member.Desc: DMD project data does not match the actual DMD

Member.Name: PadUsedInEcdSystem
Member.Desc: PAD cannot be used to drive SSI or DMD in ECD system

Member.Name: WriteToStorageApplyAtStartup
Member.Desc: Write To Storage Apply At Startup

Member.Name: AspectNative
Member.Desc: Image size maintains the aspect ratio of source and fills the DMD in at least one direction

Member.Name: Aspect43
Member.Desc: Image size maintains the aspect ratio of 4:3 and fills the DMD in at least one direction

Member.Name: Aspect169
Member.Desc: Image size maintains the aspect ratio of 16:9 and fills the DMD in at least one direction

Member.Name: Auto
Member.Desc: Decision to enable XPR is based on source resolution

Member.Name: On
Member.Desc: XPR should always be turned on

Member.Name: Off
Member.Desc: XPR should always be turned off

Member.Name: Fixed
Member.Desc: Fixed output frame rate range of 47-63Hz.

Member.Name: Sync1X
Member.Desc: FRC in sync with the incoming frame rate.

Member.Name: Sync2X
Member.Desc: FRC doubles the incoming frame rate.

Member.Name: Sync3X
Member.Desc: FRC triples the incoming frame rate.

Member.Name: Pulse
Member.Desc: Transition low-to-high (polarity dependent) w/reference signal's rising edge for configurable duration

Member.Name: Passthru
Member.Desc: Follow reference signal's waveform (pulse mode with reference signal defined duration)

Member.Name: Blanking
Member.Desc: Use only blanking time for updates

Member.Name: Disable
Member.Desc: Always disable OSD and freeze display when making updates

Member.Name: NotUsed
Member.Desc: Used for setting the DynamicBlack PWM port when DB PWM is not used

Member.Name: OneSpkOneRev
Member.Desc: Follow aux bit for a single spoke in a single color wheel revolution

Member.Name: OneSpkAllRevs
Member.Desc: Follow aux bit for a single spoke in all color wheel revolutions

Member.Name: AllSpksOneRev
Member.Desc: Follow aux bit for a single color wheel revolution

Member.Name: AllSpksAllRevs
Member.Desc: Follow aux bit for all spokes in all color wheel revolutions

Member.Name: Terminated
Member.Desc: Image Capture Terminated because of error or Timeout

Member.Name: BufferWriteInProgress
Member.Desc: External Image is being written into the internal DRAM Splash buffer

Member.Name: BufferWriteComplete
Member.Desc: Image is successfully captured into internal DRAM Splash buffer

Member.Name: FlashWriteInProgress
Member.Desc: Image is being programmed into the Flash memory

Member.Name: FlashWriteComplete
Member.Desc: Image is successfully programmed into Flash memory

Member.Name: ClrRed
Member.Desc: ChannelA=0, ChannelB=1023, ChannelC=0 for RGB sources. YUV sources will be converted.

Member.Name: ClrGreen
Member.Desc: ChannelA=1023, ChannelB=0, ChannelC=0 for RGB sources. YUV sources will be converted.

Member.Name: ClrBlue
Member.Desc: ChannelA=0, ChannelB=0, ChannelC=1023 for RGB sources. YUV sources will be converted.

Member.Name: ClrCyan
Member.Desc: ChannelA=1023, ChannelB=0, ChannelC=1023 for RGB sources. YUV sources will be converted.

Member.Name: ClrMagenta
Member.Desc: ChannelA=0, ChannelB=1023, ChannelC=1023 for RGB sources YUV sources will be converted.

Member.Name: ClrYellow
Member.Desc: ChannelA=1023, ChannelB=1023, ChannelC=0 for RGB sources. YUV sources will be converted.

Member.Name: ClrWhite
Member.Desc: ChannelA=1023, ChannelB=1023, ChannelC=1023 for RGB sources. YUV sources will be converted.

Member.Name: ClrBlack
Member.Desc: ChannelA=0, ChannelB=0, ChannelC=0 for RGB sources. YUV sources will be converted.

Member.Name: Clr75Blue25Black
Member.Desc: 75% of the line is Blue, 25% is black

Member.Name: Clr25Blue75Black
Member.Desc: 25% of the line is Blue, 75% is black

Member.Name: VertPackedHalf
Member.Desc: Over Under (vertically packed) half resolution format.

Member.Name: VertPackedFull
Member.Desc: Over Under (vertically packed) full resolution format.

Member.Name: HorizPackedHalf
Member.Desc: Side by Side (horizontally packed) half resolution format.

Member.Name: HorizPackedFull
Member.Desc: Side by Side (horizontally packed) full resolution format.

Member.Name: LeftEye
Member.Desc: VSync separated sources only, Left Eye is 1st frame in 3D image pair.

Member.Name: RightEye
Member.Desc: VSync separated sources only, Right Eye is 1st frame in 3D image pair.

Member.Name: DlpReady
Member.Desc: Single colored line(s) encoding. Must specify with @ref SRC 3D Signature t.

Member.Name: LrEmbeddedEncoding
Member.Desc: L/R reference is embedded in video data. (see @ref SRC 3D LR Encoding t)

Member.Name: NoDownsample
Member.Desc: Down Sample Operation disabled (data pass through unmodified).

Member.Name: DownsampleFirstPxl
Member.Desc: Down Sample Operation enabled. Select First Data Sample Positions from Sample Position Reference.

Member.Name: DownsampleSecondPxl
Member.Desc: Down Sample Operation enabled. Select Second Data Sample Positions from Sample Position Reference.

Member.Name: PortAEvenBOdd
Member.Desc: Dual-Port FPD (AB), if PortA carries Even and PortB carries Odd data

Member.Name: PortAOddBEven
Member.Desc: Dual-Port FPD (AB), if PortA carries Odd and PortB carries Even data

Member.Name: InvalidMode
Member.Desc: Not a valid FPD-Link data mode or mode is not used

Member.Name: Parallel
Member.Desc: Parallel Video on Port A, B and C

Member.Name: Bt656
Member.Desc: BT656 Port using GPIO 77 through GPIO 87

Member.Name: Src30Bits
Member.Desc: A total of 30 bits with 10-bits on each of R, G ,B or Y, Cb, Cr data on channels A, B and C.

Member.Name: Src24Bits
Member.Desc: A total of 24 bits with 8-bits on each of R, G, B or Y, Cb, Cr data on channels A, B and C.

Member.Name: Src20Bits
Member.Desc: A total of 20 bits with 10-bits each of Y, (CbCr) data on any A, B, or C channel with the unused input channel (Channel C) being a 'don't care'.

Member.Name: Src16Bits
Member.Desc: A total of 16 bits with 8-bits each of Y, (CbCr) data on any A, B, or C channel with the unused input channel (Channel C) being a 'don't care'.

Member.Name: ExpandedHdmi
Member.Desc: A total of 20 bits with 10-bits each of Y, (CrCb) on channel A and B respectively, Aout = Ain(9:2) + Cin(5:4); Bout = Bin(9:2) + Cin(9:8). Cout = 0

Member.Name: Src10Bits
Member.Desc: A total of 10 bits of formatted BT-656 type Y/CbCr data stream.

Member.Name: Src08Bits
Member.Desc: A total of 08 bits of formatted BT-656 type Y/CbCr data stream.

Member.Name: PortWidthBasedOnMode
Member.Desc: Only used for VBO and FPD sources to select width based on mode

Member.Name: PortSyncNoinv
Member.Desc: Input port sync is not modified(passed through).

Member.Name: AlfSync
Member.Desc: ALF Sync is selected as the Port sync source. Use when Autolock is used for source detection.

Member.Name: DecodedSync
Member.Desc: Applicable for Topfield only. The TopField is decoded from HSync and VSync.

Member.Name: SrcVbo5BytesMode
Member.Desc: 12bit mode (=5Byte mode)(12bit mode is reduced internally for 10bit processing)

Member.Name: ModeNone
Member.Desc: Not a valid V-by-one data mode or mode is not used

Member.Name: YellowIr
Member.Desc: SSI SPI Yellow/ IR for PWM systems.

Member.Name: CyanSense
Member.Desc: SSI CYAN for SPI SENSE for PWM systems

Member.Name: MagentaFrequency
Member.Desc: SSI MAGENTA for SPI / Frequency for PWM systems

Member.Name: TpgHorzrampStep0P5
Member.Desc: Gray value Increment by 2 every 4 pixel

Member.Name: TpgHorzrampStep1P0
Member.Desc: Gray value Increment by 1 every pixel

Member.Name: TpgHorzrampStep2P0
Member.Desc: Gray value Increment by 2 every pixel

Member.Name: TpgHorzrampStep0P25
Member.Desc: Gray value Increment by 1 every 4 pixel

Member.Name: TpgVertrampStep1P0
Member.Desc: Gray value Increment by 1 every line

Member.Name: TpgVertrampStep2P0
Member.Desc: Gray value Increment by 2 every line

Member.Name: TpgVertrampStep4P0
Member.Desc: Gray value Increment by 4 every line

Member.Name: CwPhaseLock
Member.Desc: Color wheel phase lock interrupt handler

Member.Name: Vsync
Member.Desc: VSYNC. This VSync drives the Display SubSystem.

Member.Name: None
Member.Desc: Parity bit is neither transmitted or checked

Member.Name: Even
Member.Desc: Even parity is transmitted and checked

Member.Name: Odd
Member.Desc: Odd parity is transmitted and checked

Member.Name: Noninv
Member.Desc: Supply non-inverted version of UART RXD input

Member.Name: Inv
Member.Desc: Supply inverted version of UART RXD input

Member.Name: Rxd
Member.Desc: UART x.RXD is sourced by UART x RXD pin

Member.Name: Lampstat
Member.Desc: UART x.RXD is sourced by LAMPSTAT pin

Member.Name: RedChMidLevelCalibratedOffset
Member.Desc: Red Ch Mid Level Calibrated Offset

Member.Name: BlueChMidLevelCalibratedOffset
Member.Desc: Blue Ch Mid Level Calibrated Offset

Member.Name: Group1NumSavedModesReadOnly
Member.Desc: Group 1 Num Saved Modes Read Only

Member.Name: Group2NumSavedModesReadOnly
Member.Desc: Group 2 Num Saved Modes Read Only

Member.Name: AsmSampleClockFreqInkHz
Member.Desc: ASM Sample Clock Freq Ink Hz

Member.Name: Successful
Member.Desc: A boolean value indicating if the command execution was successful.

Member.Name: MaskBits1
Member.Desc: Select Pattern for Gpio Pins from 31-0 for which a logic value will be applied, (b=0) Logic value will not be applied to the pin, (b=1) A logic value, supplied by Bit_Pattern will be applied

Member.Name: MaskBits2
Member.Desc: Select Pattern for Gpio Pins from 63-32 for which a logic value will be applied, (b=0) Logic value will not be applied to the pin, (b=1) A logic value, supplied by Bit_Pattern will be applied

Member.Name: MaskBits3
Member.Desc: Select Pattern for Gpio Pins from 95-64 for which a logic value will be applied, (b=0) Logic value will not be applied to the pin, (b=1) A logic value, supplied by Bit_Pattern will be applied

Member.Name: LogicValue1
Member.Desc: Logic values to be applied to the selected pins from 31-0

Member.Name: LogicValue2
Member.Desc: Logic values to be applied to the selected pins from 63-32

Member.Name: LogicValue3
Member.Desc: Logic values to be applied to the selected pins from 95-64

Member.Name: MasterDisplayColorGamutRedX
Member.Desc: Master Display Color Gamut Red x

Member.Name: MasterDisplayColorGamutRedY
Member.Desc: Master Display Color Gamut Red y

Member.Name: MasterDisplayColorGamutGreenX
Member.Desc: Master Display Color Gamut Green x

Member.Name: MasterDisplayColorGamutGreenY
Member.Desc: Master Display Color Gamut Green y

Member.Name: MasterDisplayColorGamutBlueX
Member.Desc: Master Display Color Gamut Blue x

Member.Name: MasterDisplayColorGamutBlueY
Member.Desc: Master Display Color Gamut Blue y

Member.Name: MasterDisplayColorGamutWhiteX
Member.Desc: Master Display Color Gamut White x

Member.Name: MasterDisplayColorGamutWhiteY
Member.Desc: Master Display Color Gamut White y

Member.Name: HasSubaddr
Member.Desc: SubAddress Present - 0 = No sub-addr present; 1 = sub-addr present

Member.Name: TopLeftX
Member.Desc: X position of the top left corner

Member.Name: TopLeftY
Member.Desc: Y position of the top left corner

Member.Name: TopRightX
Member.Desc: X position of the top right corner

Member.Name: TopRightY
Member.Desc: Y position of the top right corner

Member.Name: BottomLeftX
Member.Desc: X position of the bottom left corner

Member.Name: BottomLeftY
Member.Desc: Y position of the bottom left corner

Member.Name: BottomRightX
Member.Desc: X position of the bottom right corner

Member.Name: BottomRightY
Member.Desc: Y position of the bottom right corner

Member.Name: StartAddress
Member.Desc: Start Address from which data is to be written

Member.Name: NumberOfWords
Member.Desc: Number of words to be written

Member.Name: NumberOfBytesPerWord
Member.Desc: The number of bytes per word

Member.Name: IsClockPolarityPositive
Member.Desc: 0 = Clock Polarity Negative 1 = Clock Polarity Positive

Member.Name: IsExternalDataEnable
Member.Desc: 0 = External Data Disabled 1 = External Data Enabled

Member.Name: IsOffsetBinary
Member.Desc: 0 = Incoming data is signed 2s complement; typical for RGB sources 1 = offset binary; typical for YUV sources

Member.Name: IsTopFieldInvertedAtScaler
Member.Desc: 0 = Top field not inverted at scaler 1 = Top field inverted at scaler

Member.Name: BottomFieldFirstLine
Member.Desc: Bottom Field First Line - Applicable for Interlaced Sources only. This term specifies the first(starting) active line in the Bottom Field. Valid range is TopFieldFirstLine to Active Number of Lines per Frame. For Field dependent framing, BottomFieldFirstLine >= TopFieldFirstLine ( = Active area.FirstLine)

Member.Name: PixelClockFreqInKiloHertz
Member.Desc: Pixel Clock Freq In Kilo Hertz

Member.Name: OffsetRed
Member.Desc: Offset Red - Also referred to as Black Level Adjustment. Range: -256 to 255.75 in signed 8.2 format(sign + 8 integer and 2 fractional bits). Adjusts the black level for the removal of HW induced bias and/or a pedestal embedded in the Source. For changing only the offset, call the SetImageOffset command.

Member.Name: DownSampleConfiguration
Member.Desc: Down Sample Configuration - Configures the HW for downsampling. When downsampling is enabled, the PixelClockFreqInkHz is halved by the ASIC. This affects other parameters in the SRC structure such as TotalArea.PixelsPerLine, ActiveArea.PixelsPerLine and ActiveArea.FirstPixel.

Member.Name: IsClockPolarityPositive
Member.Desc: 0 = Data is clocked in on falling edge of the port clock 1 = Data is clocked in on rising edge of the port clock

Member.Name: IsExternalDataEnable
Member.Desc: 0 = External Data Enable is not used 1 = External Data Enable is used (typical for digital sources)

Member.Name: IsOffsetBinary
Member.Desc: 0 = Incoming data is signed 2s complement; typical for RGB sources 1 = offset binary; typical for YUV sources

Member.Name: IsTopFieldInvertedAtScaler
Member.Desc: 0 = Top field not inverted at scaler 1 = Top field inverted at scaler

Member.Name: BottomFieldFirstLine
Member.Desc: Bottom Field First Line - Applicable for Interlaced Sources only. This term specifies the first(starting) active line in the Bottom Field. Valid range is TopFieldFirstLine to Active Number of Lines per Frame. For Field dependent framing, BottomFieldFirstLine >= TopFieldFirstLine ( = Active area.FirstLine)

Member.Name: PixelClockFreqInKiloHertz
Member.Desc: Pixel Clock Freq In Kilo Hertz

Member.Name: ColorSpaceConvCoeffs0
Member.Desc: Color Space Conversion coefficient 0 - Coefficients used for converting YUV sources to RGB. For RGB Sources, this should be an Identity Matrix. All coefficients are defined to be signed, 2's complement values with 2 significant bits and 10 fractional bits (s2.10). For example, 1.0 = 0x0400.

Member.Name: OffsetRed
Member.Desc: Offset Red - Also referred to as Black Level Adjustment. Range: -256 to 255.75 in signed 8.2 format(sign + 8 integer and 2 fractional bits). Adjusts the black level for the removal of HW induced bias and/or a pedestal embedded in the Source. For changing only the offset, call the SetImageOffset command.

Member.Name: MaskMemTstPassed
Member.Desc: Memory test of internal DRAM passed

Member.Name: MaskDadThermFlt
Member.Desc: DAD Thermal Fault(Applicable if DAD present)

Member.Name: MaskDadVoltFlt
Member.Desc: DAD Voltage Fault(Applicable if DAD present)

Member.Name: MaskDadCurrentFlt
Member.Desc: DAD Current Fault(Applicable if DAD present)

Member.Name: Width
Member.Desc: Width of each box. Range: 0 to Horizontal Resolution.

Member.Name: Height
Member.Desc: Height of each box. Range: 0 to Vertical Resolution.

Member.Name: Period
Member.Desc: Horizontal and vertical distance between lines. Valid values are: 3, 7, 15, 31, 63, 127, 255,511, 1023.

Member.Name: DoubleLineMode
Member.Desc: Double Line Mode ControlFALSE = Normal Operation: [ 1:1 Line Counting Diagonals ] TRUE = Double Line Mode: [ 1:1 horizontal, 1:2 Vertical Stretch Diagonals ]

Member.Name: Distance
Member.Desc: Horizontal and Vertical distance between lines. Valid values are 3, 7, 15, 31, 63, 127,255, 511 and 1023.

Member.Name: RampStepWidth
Member.Desc: The width of each ramp step.

Member.Name: RampStepInc
Member.Desc: The change in value of each ramp step.

Member.Name: VerticalWidth
Member.Desc: Thickness of horizontal line. Range: 0 to Vertical Resolution

Member.Name: VerticalSpace
Member.Desc: Distance between horizontal lines. Range: 0 to Vertical Resolution

Member.Name: Width
Member.Desc: Width of each line (Range 0 to Vertical Resolution)

Member.Name: Space
Member.Desc: Space between the lines (Range 0 to Vertical Resolution)
